candidate_paradigm.primary_mechanism: B(H) = bounded-linear-operator algebra on Hilbert space H (NON-COMMUTATIVE C*-algebra under operator product + adjoint *-involution + operator-norm). Density operators D(H) = {ρ ∈ B(H) : ρ ≥ 0, Tr ρ = 1} = quantum states. CPTP map Φ: B(H_A) → B(H_B) defined by (1) LINEARITY: Φ(αρ + βσ) = αΦ(ρ) + βΦ(σ); (2) COMPLETE POSITIVITY (CP): for all integer n ≥ 1 and all ρ ∈ B(H_A ⊗ ℂ^n) with ρ ≥ 0, (Φ⊗id_n)(ρ) ≥ 0 — STRICTLY STRONGER than mere positivity; (3) TRACE PRESERVATION (TP): Tr Φ(ρ) = Tr ρ for all ρ. THREE EQUIVALENT REPRESENTATIONS (Stinespring-Kraus-Choi triadic equivalence): (i) STINESPRING DILATION (Stinespring 1955): ∃ isometry V: H_A → H_B ⊗ H_E (E = environment/ancilla Hilbert space, dim H_E ≤ dim H_A · dim H_B) such that Φ(ρ) = Tr_E(V ρ V†); equivalently ∃ unitary U on H_A ⊗ H_E and ancilla state |0⟩_E such that Φ(ρ) = Tr_E(U(ρ ⊗ |0⟩⟨0|_E) U†); (ii) KRAUS OPERATOR-SUM (Kraus 1971): ∃ finite or countable family {K_k} ⊂ B(H_A, H_B) with Σ_k K_k† K_k = I_A such that Φ(ρ) = Σ_k K_k ρ K_k† for all ρ; rank ≤ dim H_A · dim H_B; non-unique (Kraus operators related by partial isometry); (iii) CHOI-JAMIOLKOWSKI (Choi 1975): bijection Φ ↦ C_Φ := (Φ ⊗ id_{H_A})(|Ω⟩⟨Ω|) where |Ω⟩ = Σ_i |i⟩|i⟩ ∈ H_A ⊗ H_A is unnormalized maximally-entangled state; Φ is CP iff C_Φ ≥ 0 (positive-semidefinite); Φ is TP iff Tr_{H_B}(C_Φ) = I_{H_A} (partial-trace-reduction equals identity on input). LINDBLAD-GKLS continuous-time generator (Lindblad 1976 / Gorini-Kossakowski-Sudarshan 1976): dρ/dt = ℒ(ρ) where ℒ(ρ) = -i[H,ρ] + Σ_j (L_j ρ L_j† - ½{L_j† L_j, ρ}); H = Hermitian Hamiltonian, {L_j} = Lindblad jump operators; e^{tℒ} is CPTP for all t ≥ 0; characterizes Markovian quantum dynamics. DIAMOND NORM ‖Φ - Ψ‖_◇ := sup_ρ ‖(Φ⊗id)(ρ) - (Ψ⊗id)(ρ)‖_1 = operationally-meaningful distance on CPTP maps (Kitaev 1997). EXAMPLES: (a) UNITARY evolution Φ(ρ) = U ρ U† (single Kraus operator U); (b) MEASUREMENT trace-out Φ(ρ) = Σ_k Tr(M_k ρ) |k⟩⟨k| where {M_k} = POVM; (c) DEPOLARIZING channel Φ(ρ) = (1-p)ρ + p I/d; (d) AMPLITUDE-DAMPING Φ(ρ) on qubit with Kraus K_0 = diag(1,√(1-γ)), K_1 = √γ |0⟩⟨1|. CONSEQUENCES: quantum error correction (Knill-Laflamme 1997 conditions are CPTP-language), quantum capacity (Shor-Smolin-Devetak quantum-channel coding theorem), ENTANGLEMENT-BREAKING channels (Horodecki-Shor-Ruskai 2003), quantum Stinespring-dilation gives universal quantum-circuit purification.
